relations.evidence: genome-wide association studies independently implicated clusterin (CLU) as putative novel Alzheimer disease risk loci
genome-wide association studies independently implicated complement receptor 1 (CR1) as putative novel Alzheimer disease risk loci
genome-wide association studies independently implicated PICALM as putative novel Alzheimer disease risk loci
rs541458 in PICALM was shown to have an effect on CSF protein levels
the AD risk allele is associated with decreased CSF Abeta42 levels (2-tailed P = .002)
CSF biomarker analyses provide a first insight into the potentially predominant pathogenetic mechanisms underlying the association between AD risk and PICALM
CSF biomarker analyses provide a first insight into the potentially predominant pathogenetic mechanisms underlying the association between AD risk and PICALM
test the impact of these markers on cerebrospinal fluid (CSF)-Abeta42 and total-tau protein levels
